Augmented.WrongSystem: Cyberpunk 2020: Augmented Edition requires the Cyberpunk 2020 system to be active.

CYBERPUNK.SaveNotOwned: You don't own {name} — only its owner or the GM can make this save.
CYBERPUNK.StabilizeNoGM: Stabilization succeeded, but no GM is connected to record it.
CYBERPUNK.StabilizeRelayed: Stabilization recorded — the GM will confirm it.
CYBERPUNK.ParryDeclareBody: The next incoming melee attack is automatically blocked. Parry is consumed on first use. The parrying character takes −3 to all other actions this turn. (CP2020 p.102)
CYBERPUNK.ActNowBody: Use the character sheet to fire weapons or take actions.
CYBERPUNK.SuppFireNoToken: Suppressive fire: the attacker's token isn't on the active scene, so the fire zone can't be placed. Drop the attacker's token on the canvas (or target tokens manually and use the evasion prompts).
CYBERPUNK.SuppFireZoneFail: Could not place the suppressive fire zone.
CYBERPUNK.FireZoneNotFound: Fire zone not found — it may have been removed.
CYBERPUNK.NoTokensOnScene: No tokens on the current scene.
CYBERPUNK.NoTokenTargeted: No token is targeted. Right-click a token → Target (or hover + T), then click Apply Damage again.
CYBERPUNK.TakeAimTitle: Take Aim (+1 to hit per consecutive round, max +3)
CYBERPUNK.TakeAimTitleActive: Aiming ({n}/3 rounds). Click to increment. Click at 3 to reset.
CYBERPUNK.WaitActTitle: Take delayed action — announce you are acting now
CYBERPUNK.WaitForTurnBtnTitle: Wait for Turn — skip this slot and act after a chosen combatant
CYBERPUNK.DodgeTitle: Declare Dodge (−2 to attacker's melee roll, costs your action)
CYBERPUNK.DodgeTitleActive: Dodging (−2 to attacker's melee roll this round) — click to cancel
CYBERPUNK.ParryTitle: Declare Parry (block next melee attack, −3 to own other actions)
CYBERPUNK.ParryTitleActive: Parrying (next melee attack blocked) — click to cancel
CYBERPUNK.StillHeldBody: 🤜 {name} is still held by {holder}. Can only attempt Escape this turn.
CYBERPUNK.GrappledReminderBody: 🤜 {name} is grappled by {grappler}. Must escape or the grappler may Hold/Choke/Throw freely.
CYBERPUNK.ExplosionTemplateNotFound: Explosion template not found — it may have been removed.
CYBERPUNK.NoTokensInBlast: No tokens in the blast (or all behind cover).
CYBERPUNK.ScatterBody: 🎲 Scatter — {dir}{drift}. Reposition if needed, then Confirm Blast.
CYBERPUNK.SpreadFireNoToken: Spread fire: the attacker's token isn't on the active scene, so the pattern can't be placed.
CYBERPUNK.SpreadTemplateNotFound: Spread template not found — it may have been removed.
CYBERPUNK.NoTokensInSpread: No tokens in the spread pattern (or all behind cover).
CYBERPUNK.AddActionTitle: Manual Action — mark an action not tracked automatically (e.g., reload, mount/dismount)
CYBERPUNK.DamageSentWaiting: Damage sent — waiting for GM to apply.
CYBERPUNK.LimbWoundDeathSaveClause: Immediate Death Save required at Mortal 0.

Fumble.AutoWeaponOnlyJam: Automatic weapons: fumble table is ignored; only a Reliability jam check is made.
Fumble.DischargeApplied: Result: the weapon discharged (ammo is now 0).
Fumble.DischargeNotApplied: Result: lucky you, it didn’t discharge - just a misfire

AutoNotice.SettingsHint: Open Game Settings → Configure Settings → System to review combat options.

CYBERPUNK.ExplosionConfirmSummary: Blast radius {radius}m, base damage {damage}, full damage within {within}m.
CYBERPUNK.ExplosionConfirmHint: If the throw missed, click Scatter to roll where it really lands; otherwise reposition for cover and click Confirm. Damage falls off by distance.
CYBERPUNK.SpreadConfirmHint: Aim the pattern, then click Confirm. Everyone in the straight path is hit (CP2020 p.108).
CYBERPUNK.DeathSaveAutoResult: ☠ AUTOMATIC DEATH — Death Save threshold is 0 (roll ≤ 0 on 1d10 is impossible). {name} dies.
CYBERPUNK.DeathSaveResultWin: ✅ SURVIVED (rolled {result} ≤ {threshold}) — {name} clings to life. Another save required next turn.
CYBERPUNK.DeathSaveResultLose: ☠ DIED (rolled {result} > {threshold}) — {name} dies at end of this turn. Call Trauma Team.
CYBERPUNK.StabilizeHint: Stabilize: TECH + Medical + 1d10 ≥ total damage ({damage} pts)
CYBERPUNK.TargetPickPrompt: 🎯 Target a token on the canvas:
CYBERPUNK.TargetPickNoneHint: No token targeted yet. Right-click a token → Target, or hover and press T, then click Use Canvas Target.
CYBERPUNK.HeadWoundAutoDeath: ☠ AUTOMATIC DEATH — A head wound of more than 8 points kills automatically (CP2020 p.103). {name} dies. Call Trauma Team.
CYBERPUNK.DeathSaveAutoWarn: ⚠ Automatic death — threshold is 0, no roll can succeed.
CYBERPUNK.DeathSaveNeed: Must roll ≤ {threshold} on 1d10 to survive this turn
CYBERPUNK.SuppPlacementPlaced: The fire zone template has been placed on the canvas.
CYBERPUNK.SuppPlacementHint: Rotate the template to aim, then click Confirm Fire Zone to issue evasion prompts to tokens in the zone. The zone persists until the next round.
CYBERPUNK.StunSaveNeed: Must roll ≤ {threshold} on 1d10 to stay conscious
CYBERPUNK.SuppEvasionEvaded: ✅ EVADED ({total} > {dc}) — {name} clears the fire zone.
CYBERPUNK.SuppEvasionCaught: ❌ CAUGHT ({total} ≤ {dc}) — {name} takes hits! Rolling damage...
CYBERPUNK.StabilizeResultWin: ✅ STABILIZED — Roll total: {breakdown} ≥ {damage}. No further Death Saves required.
CYBERPUNK.StabilizeResultLose: ❌ FAILED — Roll total: {breakdown} < {damage}. Death Saves continue next turn.
CYBERPUNK.StabilizeResultNote: If new damage is taken, stabilization is lost and Death Saves restart.
CYBERPUNK.StabilizeDialogIntro: TECH + Medical Skill + 1d10 must equal or exceed {damage} total damage.
CYBERPUNK.StabilizeMedLabel: Medical Skill (Medical Tech or First Aid)
CYBERPUNK.StunSaveResultWin: ✅ SUCCESS (rolled {result} ≤ {threshold}) — {name} stays in the fight.
CYBERPUNK.StunSaveRecoveryNote: Can recover by rolling a successful Stun Save on a subsequent turn.
CYBERPUNK.SuppEvasionPromptRoll: Roll Athletics + REF + 1d10 to evade the fire zone.
CYBERPUNK.SuppEvasionPromptHint: REF {ref}, Athletics {ath} — must beat DC {dc} or take hits.
CYBERPUNK.WaitNoOneAfter: {name} is already the last active combatant this round — there is no one to wait after.
CYBERPUNK.WaitingBody: Acting after {target}. A prompt will appear when their turn ends.
CYBERPUNK.DodgeDeclareBody: All incoming melee attacks this round are at −2 to the attacker's roll. Dodge clears at the start of {name}'s next turn. (CP2020 p.102)
CYBERPUNK.ActionPenaltyClause: Penalty: {penalty} to all rolls this round.
CYBERPUNK.YourMomentBody: {name} just acted. Click ⚡ in the tracker to announce your delayed action, then fire from your character sheet.
CYBERPUNK.AcidExpiredBody: ⚗ {name} — Acid effect at {location} expired. Armor SP degradation complete.
CYBERPUNK.GasCloudPlacedBody:
Gas cloud placed on canvas (radius {radius}m). All tokens within the cloud must make Stun Saves each turn (penalty {mod}).
Cloud persists for {duration} turns, then disperses automatically. GM may reposition the template to represent wind drift.
CYBERPUNK.GasCloudTurnBody: In the gas cloud: {names}. Each must make a Stun Save{penalty}.
CYBERPUNK.StabilizedLostBody: ⚠ {name} was stabilized but has taken new damage — Death Saves are required again.
CYBERPUNK.ConcussionBody: 💥 {name} — concussion ({weapon}): {permanent} permanent HP (half of {gotThrough} after BTM); the other half is stun. Soft armor −2 SP. Stun Save required.
CYBERPUNK.LimbWoundCoreDetail: {net} net damage — severed or crushed beyond recognition (CP2020 p.103).
CYBERPUNK.MovementLockedAfterAction: Movement is locked: this character has already acted this turn (Restrict Movement to Once per Turn is on).

Vehicle.MMDisabledMissile: Maximum Metal is disabled — enable it in the settings to fire guided missiles.
Vehicle.MissileNeedsTokens: Missile launch needs both the firer and the target on the canvas.
Vehicle.MissileNoCombatHint: ⚠ No active encounter — start one for automatic flight, or advance the missile manually with ▶ in the Missiles in Flight panel (Combat tab).
Vehicle.MissileLaunchedNotice: Missile launched — start an encounter for better missile controls, or advance it manually in the Combat tab's Missiles in Flight panel.
Vehicle.AntiMissileBurstBody: {weapon} will hit at HALF damage & Penetration.
Vehicle.NoCmDefeats: No countermeasure defeats this missile's homing method.
Vehicle.MissileRevealTip: Reveal this missile to its target (GM)
Vehicle.DamageDisabled: Vehicle damage automation is disabled in the settings.
Vehicle.ChemicalBurstBody: A {radius}m cloud settles over the impact.
Vehicle.IndirectNeedsTarget: Target the impact point (a token at the target's location) before firing indirect.
Vehicle.IndirectIntro: {actor} fires {weapon} indirect at {target}. A spotter corrects fire; on a miss the shell scatters from the aim point.
Vehicle.BombNeedsTarget: Target the aiming point (a token at the target's location) before bombing.
Vehicle.BombIntro: {actor} drops {weapon} on {target}. A direct hit multiplies Penetration ×5; a miss scatters with altitude.
Vehicle.DiveTurnsTip: Turns spent diving at the target; each beyond the first adds +1 to-hit (max +3) and speeds the fall.
Vehicle.AircraftSpeedTip: Power-dive speed; the bomb inherits it (halving each turn to the 175 m/turn floor).
Vehicle.AcpaMeleeOnlyAcpa: ACPA melee is for powered-armor actors (tick the ACPA box).
Vehicle.AcpaNeedTarget: Target the token you're striking first.
Vehicle.RepairOnlyAcpa: Repair is for ACPA (powered armor) only.
Vehicle.AcpaRepaired: {actor} fully repaired (frame SOP, systems & weapons restored).
Vehicle.PilotRefTip: Pilot REF, capped by the suit's Reflex/Control system (derived effective REF). Override if a different pilot is driving.
Vehicle.AcpaHeatstrokeBegins: heat build-up complete — heatstroke begins: pilot Stun/Shock Save at Serious
Vehicle.AcpaHeatstrokeWorsens: heatstroke worsens — pilot Stun/Shock Save at {level}
Vehicle.LuckHitBody: Struck by a Penetration {pen} weapon. Roll LUCK + 1d10 vs 15 (MM p.8).
Vehicle.LuckHitNote: Success = grazed (5D6, half armor). Failure = Penetration vs your Armor Value. LUCK {luck}.
Vehicle.LuckGrazed: GRAZED — {dmg} (5D6) to {loc}, armor at half SP.
Vehicle.LuckStopped: Armor stopped it (Pen {pen} ≤ AV {av}) — {dmg} (2D6) impact to {loc}; armor loses {spStripped} SP.
Vehicle.LuckPenetrated: PENETRATED (Pen {pen} − AV {av} = {diff}) — {dmg} damage to {loc}; armor destroyed.
Vehicle.ControlDisabled: Vehicle control rolls are disabled in the system settings.
Vehicle.ManeuverExamples_simple: swerve, take off / land, hover, rotate, mild turn
Vehicle.ManeuverExamples_difficult: tight turn, control a skid, emergency stop, pull out of a dive, reverse
Vehicle.ManeuverExamples_veryDifficult: bootlegger reverse, extremely tight turn, vertical aerial maneuver, regain control from a spin
Vehicle.ControlFail: ❌ FAILED — control lost (rolled below DV by {missedBy}).
Vehicle.FireNoTargetClause: (no target — apply from the chat card afterward)
Vehicle.ArcWarnStrict: ⛔ Target is to the {bearing} of the firer — outside the {arc} mount's arc. Strict arc blocks this shot: rotate the firing vehicle to face the target (Ctrl+scroll), or use a turret mount.
Vehicle.ArcWarnSoft: ⚠ Target is to the {bearing} of the firer — outside the {arc} mount's arc. You can still fire (override).
Vehicle.ShellTip: Choose the loaded round; the choice is saved on the weapon. Hi-Ex/HEAT shells set their own Penetration, burst and range-immunity.
Vehicle.FireControlTip: The vehicle's fire-control / targeting-computer bonus (system.fireControl), auto-applied. Edit to add an ad-hoc bonus on top.
Vehicle.DFBTip: ACPA Direct-Fire Bonus from the suit's Reality Interface (system.dfb), auto-applied when the suit fires its own weapons (replaces a smartgun bonus).
Vehicle.FireApplyHint: No target selected. Target the enemy token (vehicle or person), then:
Vehicle.FireMultiToken: {actor} has multiple tokens on this scene — select the one you're firing from so facing and arc use the right token.
Vehicle.ArcStrictBlock: Strict arc — target is to the {bearing} of the firer, outside the {arc} mount's arc. Rotate to bear or use a turret.
Vehicle.MissileNeedTokensDirect: Missiles need the firer and target on the canvas — resolving as a direct shot.
Vehicle.ApplyTargetOne: Target exactly one token (vehicle or person), then click Apply.

CYBERPUNK.IpTrackerFooter: Queue rows come from skill rolls. Enter IP (or tick success), then Apply to release pending IP to players. Pending IP is hidden from players until applied.
CYBERPUNK.IpQueueEmpty: No skill rolls waiting. Rolls appear here as players use skills.
CYBERPUNK.IpThrottleReset: IP throttle reset for a new cycle.
CYBERPUNK.IpLockGmOnly: Only the GM can change the skill lock in this mode.
CYBERPUNK.IpPendingPip: Pending IP (GM only, hidden from players until applied)
CYBERPUNK.AmmoBuyAtShop: Ammunition must be purchased at a shop — see your GM.
CYBERPUNK.AmmoBuyFailed: Could not stock the ammunition; your eurobucks were refunded.
CYBERPUNK.AmmoBuyInsufficientFunds: Not enough eurobucks: a box costs {cost}eb but you only have {funds}eb.
CYBERPUNK.AmmoBuyNoActor: This ammo locker must be owned by a character to buy ammunition.
CYBERPUNK.AmmoRoundsPerBox: Rounds per box (the buy quantity is in boxes)
CYBERPUNK.CatalogBookVisibleHint: Eye on = players can see & buy this book's items in the catalog. Eye off = GM-only. (This is catalog-wide visibility; stock custom shops to sell per-shop.)
CYBERPUNK.CatalogBooksEyeHint: Click a book's eye to show or hide its items from players. The filters here only change your own view.
CYBERPUNK.CatalogFooter: Prices are the Core catalog cost. Ammo uses the Buy Ammo button. Cyberware charges surgery + rolls Humanity on purchase. Clothing prices by style.
CYBERPUNK.CyberBoughtUninstalled: Cyberware: {actor} bought “{item}” for {cost}eb — not installed (no surgery / Humanity yet).
CYBERPUNK.CyberInstalledSummary: Cyberware: {actor} installed “{item}” ({surgery} surgery, {charged}eb). Humanity loss {loss}, surgical damage {dmg}.
CYBERPUNK.CyberSurgeryFunds: Not enough eurobucks for surgery: costs {cost}eb, you have {funds}eb.
CYBERPUNK.ShopAlreadyStocked: That item is already in the shop.
CYBERPUNK.ShopBuildFooter: Search the catalog and ＋Add items to stock this shop. Set a per-item price (blank = catalog), Unlimited or a finite quantity, and tick Style-priced for clothing. Show to Players when ready.
CYBERPUNK.ShopBulkAddConfirm: Add {n} items to this shop? That's everything currently shown — search or filter the list first if you only want some.
CYBERPUNK.ShopBulkAddHint: Add every item currently listed below to this shop (search/filter first to narrow it).
CYBERPUNK.ShopBulkNone: Everything currently shown is already stocked.
CYBERPUNK.ShopBuyFailed: Purchase failed — you were not charged.
CYBERPUNK.ShopBuyerNeeded: Select a token or assign yourself a character to buy for.
CYBERPUNK.ShopBuyerPickHint: Choose which of your characters is shopping.
CYBERPUNK.ShopClearVendorConfirm: Remove all {n} item(s) from this shop? This can't be undone.
CYBERPUNK.ShopClearVendorHint: Remove every item from this shop's stock.
CYBERPUNK.ShopCtxHint: Click to open · right-click for options
CYBERPUNK.ShopDeleteConfirm: Delete this shop? This can't be undone.
CYBERPUNK.ShopDisabled: Shopping is disabled — ask your GM to enable it.
CYBERPUNK.ShopDiscountHint: Shop-wide % off — adjust live (e.g. when players win a persuasion roll).
CYBERPUNK.ShopFullSearchHint: Let players search & buy the whole (allowed) catalog here, with your curated items featured. Off = players see only the items you've added.
CYBERPUNK.ShopInsufficientFunds: Not enough eurobucks for {name}: costs {cost}eb, you have {funds}eb.
CYBERPUNK.ShopManageHint: Switch back to the shop builder (GM).
CYBERPUNK.ShopNoActor: No character selected to buy for.
CYBERPUNK.ShopNonePublished: No shops are open right now.
CYBERPUNK.ShopNotAllowed: Purchasing is GM-only right now — ask your GM.
CYBERPUNK.ShopOpenDetails: Click to open this item's details
CYBERPUNK.ShopPreviewHint: Open the storefront the way players see it.
CYBERPUNK.ShopQtyHint: Stock quantity — type a number for limited stock (no need to turn ∞ off first). Use the ∞ button for unlimited.
CYBERPUNK.ShopShowToPlayersHint: Open the shop to players and post a chat link they can click to browse and buy.
CYBERPUNK.ShopStockAllApplyHint: Set every item's stock to this number (limited).
CYBERPUNK.ShopStockAllHint: Set the stock of EVERY item in this shop at once.
CYBERPUNK.ShopStyleClothingOnly: Style pricing applies to clothing only (Gear › Fashion).
CYBERPUNK.ShopStyleHint: Clothing style tier — sets this item's price (Generic ×1 to High Fashion ×4).
CYBERPUNK.ShopUnlimitedHint: Toggle unlimited (∞) stock. With ∞ on, the quantity isn't tracked; turn it off (or just type a number) for limited stock.
CYBERPUNK.ShopVendorEmpty: Drag items here, or ＋ from the list below.

SETTINGS.AugmentedCombatAutomationHint: Enable automated damage application, saving throws, and area effects for the Augmented Edition combat features. Off by default.
SETTINGS.AutomationNoticeHide: Hide the Setup / What's New notice
SETTINGS.AutomationNoticeHideHint: When checked, the Cyberpunk 2020 setup & what's-new dialog stops appearing on load for the GM. Uncheck to see it again. (The notice's own 'Don't show this again' checkbox sets this too.)
SETTINGS.RestrictMovementOncePerTurn: Combat: Restrict Movement to Once per Turn
SETTINGS.RestrictMovementOncePerTurnHint: When enabled, during an active combat a combatant may reposition freely until they take a tracked action this turn (such as firing a weapon); after acting, further movement is blocked until the next round (CP2020 p.99: one move plus one action per turn). The GM can always move tokens (override). Off by default.
SETTINGS.IpSystemHint: Off by default (free skill editing). Simple = one IP pool per character. RAW = the full per-skill Improvement Points tracker (GM-attributed awards, banked-per-skill, self-service level-ups).
SETTINGS.IpAwardModelHint: Manual = the GM types IP per skill use (RAW). Auto baseline = the GM ticks success on a queued roll and a fixed IP is granted automatically (the GM can still add bonus IP).
SETTINGS.IpAutoBaselineAmountHint: IP granted per GM-marked success when the award model is Auto baseline.
SETTINGS.IpThrottleHint: Optional secondary cap on farming one skill. Off = GM polices manually (default). Hard cap = one award per skill per Apply cycle. Diminishing = each further award on the same skill this cycle is halved.
SETTINGS.IpSkillLockModeHint: Who controls the skill-lock that prevents hand-editing skill levels/IP (rolling and confirm-button level-ups always work). Owner = the player. GM = the GM only. Mutual = a player soft-lock plus a GM hard-lock the player can't override.
SETTINGS.IpShowPendingHint: Show the GM-only “pending IP” pip on skill rows (pending IP is hidden from players until applied). Per-user.
SETTINGS.ShopShowSourceHint: Show a small badge on each shop item naming the book/source it comes from. Per-user — turn it off if you prefer cleaner rows.
SETTINGS.ShoppingEnabledHint: Master toggle for the Shopping feature. When on, a 'Shop' button appears on character sheets and shops/purchases become available. Off by default (opt-in).
SETTINGS.PlayersCanShopHint: When off, only the GM can buy; players are directed to ask the GM. Keeps gear from appearing out of thin air.
SETTINGS.ShopAllowHomebrewHint: Off by default. When on, community / non-canon / cross-system items become available in the shop, where the GM can enable individual sources for players. Core and official R.Talsorian books are unaffected by this.
SETTINGS.ShopShowSource: Show item sources in the shop
SETTINGS.PlayersCanBuyAmmoHint: When off, only the GM can buy ammo; players are told they must purchase it at a shop and should ask the GM. Keeps ammo from appearing out of thin air.
